color_profiles.name: ruby
color_profiles.cause: Cr3+ substituting for Al3+; d-d electronic transitions.

color_profiles.name: blue-sapphire
color_profiles.cause: Intervalence charge transfer between Fe2+ and Ti4+ on adjacent octahedral sites.

color_profiles.name: pink-sapphire
color_profiles.cause: Low Cr3+ concentrations; boundary with ruby is debated.

visual_notes: Gem-quality crystals prized as ruby and sapphire. Non-gem material is gray-brown and often massive. Silk inclusions produce asterism in star rubies and sapphires.

optical_effects.fluorescence: Variable; rubies often fluoresce strong red under UV; blue sapphires typically inert.
optical_effects.chatoyancy: Present in star corundums when cut en cabochon.
optical_effects.asterism: Six-rayed stars in star ruby and star sapphire from intersecting rutile needle (silk) inclusions along {0001}.
